items.title: Perth set to be slammed by 'strongest weather system of the year'

children.content: Perth and large parts of Western Australia's south are preparing for "an unusually strong and intense winter system" over the WA Day long weekend, with destructive wind gusts and heavy rainfall expected.

gallery.altText: Red dirt, lightning strike,dark clouds on the left and sunshine trying to peer through on right
Rain falling and grey skies in Albany

thumbnail.altText: Red dirt, lightning strike,dark clouds on the left and sunshine trying to peer through on right
Rain falling and grey skies in Albany
Lightning flickering in stormclouds over WA.

items.title: Man who claimed mum stole Dreamworld tragedy compensation left feeling let down

image.altText: A young man wearing glasses stands next to a tree.

children.content: A man who accused his mother of stealing compensation he received after the Dreamworld Thunder River Rapids ride disaster believes the legal system has let him down after a case against her has been discontinued by prosecutors.

items.title: Mouse plague forces return of COVID-style restrictions at country WA schools

children.content: COVID-style cleaning measures have returned as WA's ongoing mouse plague spreads across multiple country schools.

image.altText: A mouse in a plastic container full of kids dress-up clothes.

items.title: Push to vaccinate the vulnerable as WA diphtheria outbreak spreads

children.content: Health authorities in Western Australia's far north say they are battling disinformation and misconceptions amid a vaccination drive to contain a diphtheria outbreak.

image.altText: woman with dark skin receiving a needle

items.title: Why $150m from Andrew Forrest may not be enough for this Pilbara community

children.content: More than a fortnight after winning the largest native title compensation payout ever in Australia, Yindjibarndi leader Michael Woodley returns to his country.

gallery.altText: An Aboriginal man in his fifties wears a blue shirt and broad-brimmed hat.
Two men in hats survey rolling green outback bushland.
A white parrot perches on a tree branch against a blue sky.

thumbnail.altText: An Aboriginal man in his fifties wears a blue shirt and broad-brimmed hat.
Two men in hats survey rolling green outback bushland.
A white parrot perches on a tree branch against a blue sky.

items.title: Mother whose two children died suddenly calls for genetic testing

children.content: A Perth woman who lost two children to an extremely rare medical condition calls for mandatory genetic testing for children whose cause of death is uncertain.

gallery.altText: A mother with blonde hair and a white dress stands in front of the ocean holding a young baby girl in a white outfit.
A young baby boy dressed in a red and green Christmas elf outfit sits on a couch smiling and laughing.
A smiling baby girl sits on the beach with a bucket wearing a white and green outfit and hat.

items.title: Dry winter could make spring bushfire conditions in WA and NSW 'tricky'

children.content: A national bushfire outlook forecasts above-average daytime and overnight temperatures for winter and an increased risk for WA and NSW.

image.altText: Generic images of flames, equipment and RFS volunteers and staff conducting a hazard reduction burn at Tura Beach, NSW

items.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers have WA quarantine period extended

children.content: Six people who were on a cruise ship that was hit by an outbreak of hantavirus have had their quarantine period in the outer Perth suburb of Bullsbrook extended until June 23.

image.altText: The exterior of a quarantine facility on the outskirts of Perth.

items.title: WA leading the charge as batteries disrupt global energy

children.content: As the use of big batteries booms across the world, Western Australia in particular is proving that grid-scale installations are no longer a complementary technology but can play a central role in power grids.

gallery.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset

thumbnail.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset
Shipping container-like batteries.
A man in high-vis wearing a hard hat stands before rows of white shipping-container-sized batteries

items.title: Lawyers expect will disputes to escalate in coming decades

children.content: A significant proportion of us believe that an inheritance will be the key to our future financial security, but most families have not even discussed what is in the will, and 40 per cent do not have one at all, a recent survey shows.

image.altText: The third will collected by the Office of the Public Trustee Queensland.

items.title: Man who claimed mum stole Dreamworld tragedy compensation left feeling let down

children.content: A man who accused his mother of stealing compensation he received after the Dreamworld Thunder River Rapids ride disaster believes the legal system has let him down after a case against her has been discontinued by prosecutors.

image.altText: A young man wearing glasses stands next to a tree.

items.title: James Valentine remembered as 'wonderful human being' at public memorial

children.content: The veteran broadcaster's family, friends and loyal listeners gathered in Sydney Town Hall to pay tribute to the 64-year-old who died last month, two years after he was diagnosed with cancer.

image.altText: Man in a suit jacket and pants wears glasses, smiling, while holding a saxophone

items.title: No further report on missing South West swimmer as police scale back search

children.content: Water police
have wound down the search
for a
swimmer who was reported to be in distress at Koombana Bay in Bunbury yesterday.

image.altText: A white ute with writing on it and surfboards on top of tray

items.title: Specific needs 'soar' across school system over past decade, principal says

children.content: WA teachers will be taught how to better support children with higher needs amid rising rates of disability diagnoses, after a report found public schools needed to do more.

gallery.altText: Students are having a math class.
Two girls are having a class in a classroom.
A principal smiles to his students in a classroom.

thumbnail.altText: Students are having a math class.
Two girls are having a class in a classroom.
A principal smiles to his students in a classroom.

items.title: Students help raise $100,000 for hay runs amid 'heartbreaking' drought

children.content: Emergency hay deliveries to drought-stricken areas resume after thousands of dollars in donations from school students and charity groups.

image.altText: a machine is loading hay bales

items.title: What the fight over London data centre plans tells us about the AI backlash

children.content: Last year in the US alone, projects collectively worth $200 billion were scuppered or delayed as communities protested the construction of new data centres promised to deliver the AI of the future.

image.altText: A man in a vampire outfit lies on the steps of an office building with protest signs against data centres behind him.

items.title: Farnham says he will not sing on stage again ahead of tribute concert

children.content: Jimmy Barnes, Tina Arena and Human Nature will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a Melbourne tribute concert later this year.

items.title: Our critics can't stop listening to these 10 albums from May

children.content: From long-awaited comebacks to First Nations glam rock and genre-defying polymaths, May was stacked with excellent new releases.

image.altText: Collage of Genesis Owusu, Ecca Vandal, Kneecap and Mulga Bore Hard Rock.

items.title: Refinery on WA's south coast says it could replace region's diesel with biofuel

children.content: In the 1990s, Esperance was seen as a key to a major biofuels industry for Western Australia. Thirty years later, the industry has seen little change. But some say the area has untapped potential as a major fuel supplier.

gallery.altText: A field of flowering yellow canola.
A truck on a pile of wood chips at a refinery

thumbnail.altText: a petrol pump
A field of flowering yellow canola.
A truck on a pile of wood chips at a refinery

items.title: How tax rules benefit top earners by $700,000 but median earners by $5,700

children.content: Commonwealth Treasury secretary Jenny Wilkinson says the average top income earner benefited from the existing investment tax arrangements to the tune of $700,000 over their lifetime, versus just $5,700 for median income earners.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Treasury Secretary Jenny Wilkinson in treasurer's office

items.title: Government defends blocking counterterror documents from Bondi royal commission

children.content: The federal government has made a public interest immunity claim over the documents before the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, essentially arguing it is not in the public interest for the documents to be released.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair, wearing a lilac jacket, standing next to a man in a suit.

items.title: Philosopher tasked with keeping Australians safe from AI

children.content: A philosopher and Royal Australian Air Force reservist has been chosen to lead the federal government's efforts to keep Australians safe from the harms of AI.

image.altText: A young white woman standing in front of a military plane

items.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of jobs in search for white-collar workers

children.content: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly skilled white-collar workers overseas.

image.altText: A large outdoor Officeworks sign in front of large trees.

items.title: Social media group calls for change while exposing inequality in sport

children.content: Not All Clubs calls out inequality in sporting organisations across the country. This weekend, the group is calling on teams to wear red armbands in memory of the women whose lives have been claimed by gender-based violence this year.

gallery.altText: Two AFL players stand in a huddle. Both players wear two red bands on their left arm.
A women's AFL team stand in a pre-game huddle, each players is wearing two red bands on their left arm.
Someone holding a phone loaded to the Instagram profile of Not All Clubs.
